result:
I’d give everyone reliable access to high‑quality education, from early childhood through adulthood, tailored to how they learn best.

That doesn’t just mean more schools or more content online—it means:
- Every child is safe, fed, and emotionally supported enough to learn.
- Teaching is evidence-based and continuously improved, like good medicine.
- People can reskill throughout life without going into debt.
- Critical thinking, empathy, and media literacy are treated as core subjects.

If that were in place globally, it would make it easier to tackle almost every other problem—climate, conflict, health, inequality—because more people would have the tools to understand issues, cooperate, and build better systems.

content.text:
I’d give everyone reliable access to high‑quality education, from early childhood through adulthood, tailored to how they learn best.

That doesn’t just mean more schools or more content online—it means:
- Every child is safe, fed, and emotionally supported enough to learn.
- Teaching is evidence-based and continuously improved, like good medicine.
- People can reskill throughout life without going into debt.
- Critical thinking, empathy, and media literacy are treated as core subjects.

If that were in place globally, it would make it easier to tackle almost every other problem—climate, conflict, health, inequality—because more people would have the tools to understand issues, cooperate, and build better systems.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
